description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a cylindrical body and a domed top, featuring three prominent hose connection outlets—two on the sides and one at the front. The outlets are capped and secured with metal chains, which prevent the caps from being lost when removed. The hydrant is constructed from robust metal, likely cast iron or steel, and is bolted to a circular base plate with visible bolts. The surface is painted a deep red, with some areas showing subtle wear and darkened edges, suggesting exposure to outdoor elements. The top of the hydrant has a central operating nut or cap, used for opening the water valve, and the overall design is utilitarian, built for durability and high visibility in urban environments.
description: A robust red metal fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets, secured by chains, and a domed top with a central operating nut. The hydrant is bolted to a circular base and shows signs of outdoor wear.
description_view_0: This view shows the hydrant from an angle, highlighting the front outlet with its cap and chain, as well as one of the side outlets.
description_view_1: The front view displays all three hose outlets clearly, with chains attached to each cap and the central operating nut on top visible.
description_view_2: This side angle emphasizes the opposite side outlet and its cap, with the front outlet partially visible and the chain details prominent.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the hydrant's cylindrical body and both side outlets, with the front outlet not visible.
